data.title: HanesBrands Inc. beat Q2 2025 expectations, raises full-year outlook. CEO Steve Bratspies pleased with revenue growth.
data.description: HanesBrands Inc., a prominent apparel company (NYSE: HBI), released its second-quarter 2025 results from Winston-Salem, N.C. CEO Steve Bratspies commented on their success in exceeding revenue, profit, and earnings per share growth expectations for three consecutive quarters. The company's growth strategy and past transformation efforts have been key drivers of this positive performance, with a clear pathway to cost savings and input efficiency.

data.title: ADS increases quarterly cash dividend to shareholders by 13% to $0.18 per share, announced by Scott Barbour, President & CEO.
data.description: Based in Hilliard, Ohio, Advanced Drainage Systems, Inc. (NYSE: WMS) known for its innovative water management solutions, has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share for its shareholders. This marks a 13% rise from the previous year's dividend. The company's Board of Directors confirmed this increase. Scott Barbour, the President and CEO of Advanced Drainage Systems, made this announcement today.

data.title: Advanced Drainage Systems reported Q1 2026 results: Net sales up 1.8% to $829.9M, net income down 11.2% to $144.1M.
data.description: In Hilliard, Ohio, Advanced Drainage Systems, Inc. (NYSE: WMS), a key player in water management solutions for stormwater and onsite wastewater sectors, revealed its financial performance for the fiscal first quarter ending June 30, 2025. Net sales saw a rise of $14.5 million, reaching $829.9 million, marking a 1.8% increase. However, net income experienced a decrease of $18.2 million, down by 11.2% to $144.1 million. The net income per diluted share also decreased by $0.22.

data.title: Target Hospitality reports strong Q2 2025 results, raises full-year outlook. One of N. America's top providers of modular accommodations.
data.description: In The Woodlands, Texas, on August 7, 2025, Target Hospitality Corp. (NASDAQ: TH), a leading provider of modular accommodations and hospitality services in North America, announced its recent financial performance. As one of the largest companies in this sector, Target Hospitality shared its latest figures and insights, reflecting its strong position in the market.

data.title: Constellation Energy Corp. (Nasdaq: CEG) reports Q2 2025 results; CEO aims for reliability amid rising demand, mentions grid expansion.
data.description: In Baltimore, Constellation Energy Corporation (Nasdaq: CEG) released its financial report for Q2 2025. President and CEO Joe Dominguez emphasized their contributions to meeting the rising demand for electricity driven by AI, electric vehicles, and industrial expansion in the US. The company is focused on bolstering grid reliability and affordability. Strategies include adding megawatts through prolonging the lifespan of the current fleet and expediting the Crane facility.

data.title: Brookfield Corp posts robust Q2 results, plans 3:2 stock split.
data.description: In the latest report, it was disclosed that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.5%, which marks a significant decrease from the previous month's rate of 5.2%. This decline in the unemployment rate represents a total of 500,000 individuals finding employment during the month. Additionally, the report highlighted a growth in the economy by 6.8% in the last quarter, exceeding expectations and indicating a positive trend in the country's economic performance. This growth is attributed to various sectors, including manufacturing and services, which saw a notable increase in output and demand. Overall, the latest data reflects a promising outlook for the country's economy and job market.

data.title: Brilliant Earth exceeds Q2 guidance with 3.3% Y/Y net sales growth and 18% Y/Y total orders increase. Announces $0.25/share dividend.
data.description: A 3.3% year-over-year increase in net sales resulted in an 18% growth in total orders. This performance led to an upward revision in the annual net sales guidance. Additionally, a one-time dividend and distribution of $0.25 per share has been declared.

The company's June quarter guidance was in line with forecasts, leading to mixed reactions among investors. The post Fortinet Earnings Exceed Expectations. data.title: Fullpower-AI partners with Tempur Sealy for 10-year global licensing and investment, shaping future of smartbeds.
data.description: Today, in Santa Cruz, California, Fullpower-AI® revealed a significant partnership with Tempur Sealy International, a subsidiary of Somnigroup International Inc. This collaboration is set to revolutionize health and wellness technologies.

data.title: Warby Parker reports Q2 2025 results: 300 stores opened, 20 million glasses distributed. Partnering with Google for smart eyewear.
data.description: In New York, Warby Parker Inc. (NYSE: WRBY), a direct-to-consumer lifestyle brand specializing in vision, disclosed its financial performance for the quarter ending on June 30, 2025. The company highlighted key achievements, including the inauguration of their 300th store and the provision of 20 million pairs of glasses to individuals in underserved regions globally. Warby Parker also mentioned an upcoming collaboration with Google for the advancement of smart eyewear technology.

data.title: Kolibri Global Energy Inc. updates on operations in Oklahoma, including Lovina wells in Tishomingo field.
data.description: Kolibri Global Energy Inc. has shared an operations update on its recent wells in the Tishomingo field in Oklahoma. The Lovina 9-16-1H, Lovina 9-16-2H, Lovina 9-16-3H, and Lovina 9-16-4H wells, each with 100% working interest, have been effectively fracture stimulated. Currently, the wells are flowing back the fracture stimulation fluid through a controlled flowback process. The company is pleased with the progress of these wells. Kolibri is listed on the TSX as KEI and on the NASDAQ as KGEI.

data.title: United Parks & Resorts Inc. reports Q2 2025 results: 6.2 million guests, $490.2 million revenue, $80.1 million net income. Q1 attendance was 9.6 million, with $777.2 million revenue and $64.0 million net income. [Character count: 191]
data.description: United Parks & Resorts Inc. announced their financial results for Q2 and first half of fiscal year 2025. In Q2, attendance was 6.2 million guests, with total revenue of $490.2 million and net income of $80.1 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $206.3 million, with total revenue per capita decreasing to $78.64. In the first six months of 2025, attendance was 9.6 million guests, with total revenue of $777.2 million and net income of $64.0 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $273.7 million, and total revenue per capita was $80.74. The company rescued 500 animals in Q2, bringing the total aided animals to over 42,000. The Board recommended a $500 million share buyback authorization. Marc Swanson, CEO, expressed optimism for future trends and upcoming events. Management also discussed non-GAAP financial measures such as Adjusted EBITDA and notable forward-looking statements, mentioning various risks involved. For further details and the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Apogee Enterprises names new presidents for business segments, Nick Longman resigns as President of Architectural Metals.
data.description: Apogee Enterprises, Inc., a major player in architectural building products, and high-performance coated materials, declared the addition of two new business segment presidents. On August 7, 2025, Nick Longman steps down as President of the Architectural Metals Segment. Troy Johnson will succeed him in leading the segment. Apogee Enterprises, Inc. operates under the stock symbol Nasdaq: APOG.
